name: South Korea
summary: KOSPI 6,091 (+2.1%). Semiconductor-led rally — TSMC record high driving KOSPI via Samsung/SK Hynix. AI chip demand structural and independent of war. Helium cliff bear case dead. Crisis relief component ~40% (reverses on talks failure); semiconductor/AI story ~60% (durable). Current account surplus at record, exports +48.3%. KRW strengthening on DXY weakness.

price_snapshots.trigger: BIGGEST equity mover globally — triple catalyst
price_snapshots.delta_context: Surged +8.4% from 5,052 on: (1) Korean March exports +48.3% YoY record $86.1B (fundamentals ~4.5%), (2) Pezeshkian peace signal → helium relief (~2.5%), (3) global risk-on (~1.4%). Samsung +13.6%, SK hynix +11.4%. Double circuit breakers triggered on the rally (TradingKey). Won weakened to 1,503 despite equity surge (foreign selling into strength).

price_snapshots.trigger: Strongest single-day rally — de-escalation + oil crash + mean reversion
price_snapshots.delta_context: Surged +8.44% (5,070→5,479). Samsung +13%, SK Hynix +10%. Buy-side sidecar triggered within 7 minutes. Institutions net-bought 4.03T won. Most punished market (-20% March) bounced hardest.

price_snapshots.trigger: Ceasefire-day rally + helium cliff fading + AI semi cycle stacked
price_snapshots.delta_context: +7.9% intraday, close +8.4%; Samsung +13% biggest move since Dec 2001; SK Hynix broke 1M KRW

signals.content: KOSPI crashed 6.5% triggering circuit breaker — worst single-day drop since COVID. Samsung Electronics fell 8.2%. SK Hynix fell 9.1%. Driven by oil spike + helium supply concerns + global risk-off. (Sources: yfinance, Bloomberg, Reuters)

KOSPI at 5,438.87 (-0.4% daily, -10.6% monthly). Two semiconductor fabs announced reduced output due to helium allocation restrictions following Ras Laffan damage. USD/KRW at 1,508 — weakest since 2022. South Korea's energy import dependence (~70% from Gulf) makes it acutely exposed to Hormuz disruption. (Sources: yfinance, industry reports)

KOSPI triple cascade confirmed: helium→semiconductor fabs (~40% of -10.6% monthly decline, ~4.2%), Google TurboQuant AI memory compression (~25%, ~2.7%), foreign capital flight (~35%, ~3.7%). Foreigners dumped 51.4 trillion won ($37.8B) since February — largest outflow since Global Financial Crisis. Samsung foreign ownership hit 48.9% — lowest since October 2013 (12.5-year low). Won at 1,508/USD — weakest since March 2009 (17-year low). TrendForce's March 24 'two-week clock' on helium inventories means ~8 days remain before Asian chip fab inventories exhaust at full production rates. The helium cascade path (war→Qatar→helium→semiconductors→KOSPI) validated by 3 independent sources (TrendForce, Nikkei Asia, Korea International Trade Association). (Sources: yfinance, TrendForce, Korea International Trade Association)

KOSPI deepened decline to 5,281.76 (-2.9% Monday, -16.3% monthly). Foreign capital flight accelerated: ₩51.4T ($37.8B) sold since Feb — largest since GFC. Samsung foreign ownership at 48.9% (12.5-year low). Won at ₩1,512 (17-year low). Helium data conflict: Samsung claims 6 months stock but TrendForce says industry at ~6 days. SK hynix likely more vulnerable than Samsung. (Sources: yfinance, Korea International Trade Association, TrendForce, Korea Plus)

KOSPI broke 5,100 support — fell -3.4% to 5,099.97 on March 31 (LIVE data). Worst-performing major index: -18.3% monthly. SK hynix -5.8%, Samsung -3.5%. Won at ₩1,533.72 (17-year low, +1.8% daily). Triple cascade confirmed: helium (~40%), energy dependence (~25%), foreign capital flight (~35%). TrendForce helium clock: ~6-8 days to Asian fab exhaustion. Samsung claims 6-month buffer via HeRS recycling system. No confirmed production halt yet. Next level: 5,000 (psychological).

KOSPI closed at 5,052.46 (-4.26%) — worst performing major index globally in March (-19.1% monthly). Approaching bear market territory. Samsung -5.2%, SK hynix -5.2% — gap narrowed, weakening Samsung HeRS differentiation thesis. Foreign investors sold 35 trillion won in March (all-time monthly record). Won at 17-year low (₩1,524.62). Triple whammy: helium cascade (~30%), Google TurboQuant (~40%), high chip prices (~30%). KOSPI broke below 5,100 helium cascade threshold. Next level: 5,000 (psychological). CONFIRMED.

KOSPI surged +8.4% to 5,478.70 — BIGGEST equity mover globally on April 1. Triple catalyst: (1) Korean March exports +48.3% YoY to RECORD $86.1 billion, crushing consensus +44.9% — semiconductor exports on 10+ month expansion streak (fundamentals ~4.5%). (2) Pezeshkian peace signal → if war ends, Qatar Ras Laffan helium resumes, Korea's biggest existential risk removed (~2.5%). (3) Global risk-on tracking Wall Street's strongest session in months (~1.4%). Samsung +13.6%, SK hynix +11.4% — massive outperformance. Double circuit breakers triggered on the rally (TradingKey). Won weakened to ₩1,503 despite equity surge (foreign selling into strength). Fabs STILL on 50% helium allocations (CONFIRMED rationing, Gasworld). RELIEF RALLY, not resolution — all three legs fragile. Exports backward-looking, peace is single statement, helium rationing real NOW. +8.4% on top of +27.1% 3M raises exhaustion risk. If any leg breaks, KOSPI gives back 4-5%. (Sources: CNBC, Seoul Economic Daily, TradingKey, yfinance, Gasworld)

KOSPI +8.44% on April 2 (5,070 to 5,479) — strongest single-day rally in recent history. Samsung +13%, SK Hynix +10%. Buy-side sidecar triggered within 7 minutes of opening. Institutions net-bought 4.03T won; retail net-sold 3.76T won (smart money buying, retail selling into strength). Most crisis-sensitive market bounced hardest — Korea was most punished major market (-20% in March) and experienced mean reversion from extreme oversold levels. (Sources: Seoul Economic Daily, yfinance)

Relief rally drivers: (1) De-escalation signals (Pezeshkian peace offer + Trump "2-3 weeks" exit timeline) → oil crash → Korea imports virtually ALL crude from Middle East, providing ~80% of the relief. (2) Partial semiconductor relief — peace signal eases helium concern as Ras Laffan (Qatar) may resume operations, providing ~15% of rally. Samsung foreign ownership had fallen from 52.6% to 48.6% (12.5-year low) — potential for foreign re-entry if de-escalation confirmed. (Sources: Seoul Economic Daily, Bloomberg, TrendForce)

Analysts warn rally may be premature: oil still above $100, Hormuz passage unresolved, TurboQuant's HBM impact unsettled. Hana Securities: "Full KOSPI rebound hinges on confirmation of oil price stabilization and shift to foreign net buying." Fabs still on 50% helium allocations (confirmed rationing). Won at ~1,503 (weakened despite equity surge — foreign selling into strength on prior session). (Sources: Hana Securities)

KOSPI reversed from +8.44% relief rally to -4.1% at 5,254 on April 2 — the entire peace rally was a false dawn. Samsung and SK hynix both giving back yesterday's gains. The triple cascade persists: helium rationing at 50% allocations (confirmed), energy dependence, foreign capital flight. Russia reported 'poised to ease global helium crunch' (Pravda) but no confirmed supply deals. The KOSPI whiplash (+8.4% then -4.1% in 24 hours) reflects a market trading on headline sentiment, not fundamentals — the helium supply crisis and fab exhaustion window are unchanged. (Sources: yfinance, TrendForce, Pravda Japan)

WHIPLASH continues: KOSPI +2.6% to 5,369.58 on April 3 after -4.5% on April 2 and +8.4% on April 1. Three-session pattern reflects headline-driven trading, not fundamental resolution. Helium 50% allocations unchanged. Russia 'poised to ease global helium crunch' per Pravda but no confirmed deals. KOSPI operating profit hit record 245T won — but excluding Samsung/SK hynix, growth halves to 10.76% from 25.39%. Market concentration risk: the two semiconductor giants ARE the index. Korean March CPI 2.2% with petroleum +9.9% — pushing back BOK rate cut hopes. (Sources: Seoul Economic Daily, yfinance, Pravda Japan, TrendForce)

KOSPI at 5,377 (+2.7% on April 3, script data). Seoul Economic Daily: 'Foreign buyers return, lifting Samsung and SK hynix.' Samsung +4.7%, SK hynix +6.1%. Separately, SemiMedia reported South Korea helium supply expected to last through at least June — extending timeline from TrendForce's '1-2 weeks' estimate. Korean fabs still on 50% helium allocations. TrendForce production cut announcement window April 7-10 still active. Four-session KOSPI pattern: +8.4%, -4.5%, +2.6%, +2.7% = oscillating around 5,300-5,400, headline-driven. April 4 (Good Friday) markets may have been closed. (Sources: Seoul Economic Daily, SemiMedia, TrendForce, yfinance)

KOSPI -4.47% to 5,234 on April 3 — completely reversed the April 2 foreign-buyer rally (+2.9%). Samsung -5.91%, SK Hynix -6.83%, Hyundai Motor -4.61%, Doosan Enerbility -6.02%. Trump's signal that US is 'nearing completion' but will 'hit Iran extremely hard over the next 2-3 weeks' rattled global risk sentiment. Five-session KOSPI pattern: +8.4%, -4.5%, +2.6%, +2.7%, -4.47% = oscillating on headlines, not fundamentals. TrendForce production cut window (April 7-10) remains THE semiconductor trigger. Helium supply stable through June per SemiMedia but fabs still 50% allocations. Korean March CPI 2.2% with petroleum +9.9% — pushing back BOK rate cut hopes.

KOSPI rebounded to 5,458 (+0.1% / +1.36% from last report) on ceasefire hopes, partially reversing -4.47% from prior cycle. Inside TrendForce April 7-10 production cut announcement window — no Samsung or SK Hynix formal cut as of filing. ABSENCE of announcement during window is itself data: either inventories deeper than feared or companies managing through 50% helium allocations without formal cuts. South Korea claims inventory through June but TrendForce does not endorse. Helium NE Asia spot $152.70/MCF (+50-100% from pre-crisis). IRGC threat to 18 Western tech companies and UAE Stargate adds new dimension to semiconductor supply uncertainty. (Sources: Trading Economics, TrendForce, Tom's Hardware, CNBC)

KOSPI WHIPSAW STABILIZING — KOSPI +0.4% intraday April 7 (small gain — notable given the -4.47% crash from April 3). Recovered fully from prior cycle weakness on (a) Bank of Korea / SK helium rumor, (b) front-running Tuesday resolution either way. The TrendForce April 7-10 production cut announcement window has OPENED with NO Samsung or SK Hynix formal cut — Samsung 6-month buffer + HeRS 18.6%/yr saving holding the line. SMH +0.9% 1D / +0.2% 1M validates no-cliff scenario. Korean semis names benefiting from the un-pricing of the helium cliff. KRW 1,507/USD ~flat — Korean currency pressure absorbing rather than spiraling.

EVENING UPDATE: KOSPI 5,495 (+0.8%). Inside TrendForce April 7-10 window — no production cut announced. SMH +0.9% validates no-cliff scenario. IRGC Stargate AI data center threat adds new dimension to semiconductor supply chain risk — Gulf tech infrastructure now a target. Samsung and SK Hynix earnings mid-April will provide ground truth on helium impact. (Sources: yfinance, TrendForce, NewsX)

KOSPI +7.9% (CLOSE +8.4%), SAMSUNG +13%, SK HYNIX +11% — DOUBLE CATALYST [CONFIRMED]. South Korea's stock market surged on the US-Iran ceasefire, with KOSPI opening at 5,804.70 (+5.64%) and closing +8.4%. Samsung Electronics +13% (biggest single-day gain since December 2001), SK Hynix +11% (broke 1 million won per share, triggered program-trading sidecar). Two distinct catalysts stacked: (1) Ceasefire risk-on rotation back into Asian equities (~50% of move), (2) Helium-cliff fading with potential Qatar Ras Laffan restart on the 2-week horizon (~25% of move), (3) AI semiconductor cycle / HBM3E pricing power continues independent (~25%). USD/KRW -2.3% to ₩1,473.48 — KRW best-performing currency. South Korea sources 64.7% of helium from Qatar; the helium tail risk that yesterday's brief flagged as 'the dog that didn't bark' is fading. The Friday April 10 Islamabad talks failure scenario would reverse only ~50% of the rally — the helium-cliff-removal is permanent.

KOSPI closed at 5,872.34 (+6.87%) on April 8 on double catalyst: ceasefire helium relief (~50%) + Samsung Q1 earnings surprise (~30%) + broad EM risk-on (~20%). Samsung +7.38%, SK Hynix +13.65%. USD/KRW -1.4% to ₩1,478.35. April 9: KOSPI retreated ~1.4% to ~5,790. Samsung -3.1%, SK Hynix -2.4%. Record 5.4 trillion won retail selling on April 9 — classic profit-taking after monster rally. Helium relief is CONDITIONAL — physical delivery from Ras Laffan takes 3-4 weeks. By April 10 with no TrendForce production-cut announcement, helium cliff bear case is functionally dead. (Source: Seoul Economic Daily, Trading Economics, yfinance)

HELIUM BEAR CASE DEAD. TrendForce window expired April 10 — NO production cut announced. Samsung signed 3-5 year Long-Term Agreements with Linde and Air Products for helium supply (TrendForce, April 9) — structural supply chain restructuring, not emergency measures. Samsung has ~6 months helium stock + HeRS recycling system targeting 18.6% annual reduction. SK Hynix also signed LTAs. South Korea government secured 4+ months semiconductor-grade helium. China pushing domestic ASML-certified ultra-pure helium. KOSPI +1.7% (5,874), SMH +1.7%. The semiconductor helium cliff scenario is RESOLVED — supply chain adapted rather than broke. (Source: TrendForce, DigiTimes, Tom's Hardware)

KOSPI +3.0% to 6,149 — semiconductor-led risk-on rally. TSMC record high (NT$2,055, +3.27%) driving KOSPI via Samsung/SK Hynix heavyweight linkage. Q1 TSMC revenue +35% on AI chip demand (GPUs, accelerators, CoWoS packaging). MediaTek +6.17%, Hon Hai +3.75%. Helium cliff bear case dead — fabs maintained 6-week buffers without halt. Crisis relief component (~40%) reverses on talks failure; semiconductor earnings story (~60%) is durable. South Korea current account surplus at record, exports +48.3% — fundamental, not crisis-driven. DXY weakness (98.16) + BRL/KRW strengthening driving EM capital flows into Asian tech. (Sources: yfinance, TSMC quarterly data)

EVENING UPDATE: KOSPI closed 6,091 (+2.1%) — ASML earnings drove semiconductor rally. ASML raised full-year guidance to EUR36-40B (from EUR35-39B), confirming AI fab spending acceleration. Samsung +2.18%, SK Hynix +2.99% — heavyweight semis drove KOSPI. ASML's guidance raise validates that chip capex cycle is REAL and durable, not just crisis-recovery bounce. KOSPI now split ~60% semiconductor/AI story (durable) and ~40% crisis relief (reverses on talks failure). Helium cliff bear case dead — fabs maintained 6-week buffers. South Korea current account surplus at record, exports +48.3%. KRW strengthening on DXY weakness (98.24). (Sources: yfinance, ASML earnings, Seoul Economic Daily)

edges.context: KOSPI +6.87% partly on ceasefire helium relief (~50%). Fabs still on 50% allocations. Physical delivery takes 3-4 weeks. By April 10 with no production-cut announcement, bear case dead.
Korea imports virtually ALL crude from Middle East. Oil crash from $118 to $101.97 provided ~80% of KOSPI's +8.44% rally driver. Oil still above $100 is the binding risk.
Majority of Korean energy imports transit Hormuz. De-escalation signals easing Hormuz closure risk — major driver of KOSPI relief rally. Hormuz resolution = bulk of Korea's crisis recovery.

trigger_points.condition: Samsung or SK Hynix announces production halt due to helium shortage
trigger_points.mechanism: Confirmed fab shutdown would cascade to global semiconductor supply, AI infrastructure, and tech valuations worldwide. Currently only 'reduced output' reported, not full halt.
